response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and systems software developer with strong fundamentals, but his background is almost entirely in web, mobile, and application development — TypeScript, Node.js, JavaScript, and D-lang. The Principal Performance Architect role requires deep specialization in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware-software co-design, GPU architecture, and deep neural network workload optimization, none of which appear anywhere in his resume. His closest adjacent experience is low-latency application tuning at Sociomantic and some C/C++ work, but that does not bridge the gap to silicon-level performance architecture for AI accelerators.
strengths: 14+ years of software engineering experience across diverse domains satisfies the BS+8yr education threshold
Demonstrated performance-oriented work in high-throughput, low-latency systems (millions of HTTP requests/sec at Sociomantic)
Breadth of systems knowledge including embedded, C/C++, asynchronous I/O, and some hardware-adjacent work (robotic arms, wave field synthesis thesis)
concerns: Zero experience with SoC or IP modeling, which is a core required skill for this role
No background in AI/ML, deep learning workloads, GPU architecture, or neural network optimization
No hardware-software co-design or chip-level performance analysis — the entire technical profile is software application development
